Subject: [Bug 5820] URIBL_SBL from zen?
Body: http://issues.apache.org/SpamAssassin/show_bug.cgi?id=5820 ------- Additional Comments From xndd@hege.li 2008-02-11 00:40 ------- Ahem, maybe you didn't get the point. Zen includes SBL, so you can check for SBL _only_ with result 127.0.0.2. Just like RCVD_IN_SBL check zen. ------- You are receiving this mail because: ------- You are the assignee for the bug, or are watching the assignee.

Subject: Thank you for signing up
Body: Dear Honey, Thank you for signing up to receive email updates from the American Cancer Society! You‚Äôll receive updates on groundbreaking research, tips for staying healthy, and how you can get more involved to help create a world with less cancer. If you'd like help or information right away, we encourage you to explore the wealth of resources available at cancer.org or call us any time at 1-800-227-2345. You can also like us on Facebook and follow us on Twitter to join the thousands of other people like you committed to the fight against cancer. And rest assured‚Äìwe care about your privacy, and your responses will remain confidential as described in our privacy policy. You can unsubscribe from emails at any time. Sincerely,Your American Cancer Society

Sincerely,Your American Cancer Society

Subject: Looking to a better future
Body: Plus: A satirical debut, and the story of the physicist who invented the atomic bomb ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå ‚Äå Books By Deborah Dundas After a week where climate change was top of mind with thick wildfire smoke blanketing the GTA, it makes sense to look to the future ‚Äî how human nature might shape it, and how we can work together to make it better. In ‚ÄúOur Tribal Future,‚Äù University of Toronto biological anthropologist David R. Samson examines how we are naturally inclined to form groups. In a Q&A with the Star, Samson argues that ‚Äúmaladaptive tribalism‚Äù leads to negative outcomes like xenophobia and the spread of conspiracy theories. But tribalism can also be a force for good, bringing co-operation and altruism. And he should know. He is ‚Äúis one of only a handful of scientists who have done field research among both human forager societies and wild chimpanzees‚Äù and grew up in a fundamentalist cult in Indiana, our interviewer, Brian Bethune, points out. ‚ÄúFrom the beginning, the tribal drive has been bug and feature, curse and blessing,‚Äù Samson told the Star. ‚ÄúBringing it to people‚Äôs conscious awareness, I believe, is the only shot our species has of reducing intergroup conflict in the future.‚Äù There is more book news and reviews below, too, to get you started on a week of reading. Join us again next Friday for more from the world of books. Creating a better future: How to take our instinct to belong to a group and turn it into a force for good In his new book ‚ÄòOur Tribal Future,‚Äô David R. Body: Brad Bourn wrote: > > > > On Friday 08 February 2008 3:57:49 pm David C. Rankin wrote: >> I'd be glad to. Let me look your guide above and we will figure out what >> you want to add/etc and then we can drop the finished product into the wiki. > > I just went through your howto on my amd_64 machine. All worked once I > realized I needed to download the 64bit version. Subject: [ie-rant] hp pavilion issues
Body: Hi all you techies out there in ierant land. I recently formatted my hp pavillion(s7720.uk) in order to reinstall xp on it. (Vista was driving me round the bend). I'm not sure my machine was intended to run anything other than vista. Now that I've installed xp on it I am missing certain drivers. \\most importantly \\i cannot connect it to the internet which means I cannot easily search for drivers. at present I'm missing drivers for - Other PCI Bridge Device PCI Device SM Bus Controller Video Controller What I'm hoping for is that one of you is a hp expert and could help me sort this out. I'm used to Dell machines so this is proving to be a royal pain in the $%^ at the moment. To top it off the only other machine in the house is my italian flatmates spyware ridden piece of shit, wich means saving my own machine is further complicated. all help appreciated! - Richard -- I'm on the rocky road, Heading down off the mountain slope, And as my steps echo (echo) louder than before
text:
[ie-rant] hp pavilion issues

Hi all you techies out there in ierant land. I recently formatted my hp pavillion(s7720.uk) in order to reinstall xp on it. (Vista was driving me round the bend). I'm not sure my machine was intended to run anything other than vista. Now that I've installed xp on it I am missing certain drivers. \\most importantly \\i cannot connect it to the internet which means I cannot easily search for drivers. at present I'm missing drivers for - Other PCI Bridge Device PCI Device SM Bus Controller Video Controller What I'm hoping for is that one of you is a hp expert and could help me sort this out. I'm used to Dell machines so this is proving to be a royal pain in the $%^ at the moment. To top it off the only other machine in the house is my italian flatmates spyware ridden piece of shit, wich means saving my own machine is further complicated. all help appreciated! - Richard -- I'm on the rocky road, Heading down off the mountain slope, And as my steps echo (echo) louder than before

Subject: Re: [opensuse] Intel 3945 Wireless Problem opensuse 10.3 - RESOLVED!
Body: On Saturday 09 February 2008 01:58:41 pm James Knott wrote: > There's not much point in hiding the SSID, as it doesn't do much to stop > attackers. It also means that a neighbour, looking for a clear spot to > put his WiFi, might chose the same channel as yours. The best way to > get WiFi security, is to use WPA (WPA2 if possible), which is very > secure. WEP is not. I've also got my home WiFi (WPA2) on the outside > of my firewall, so SSH, with public key authentication or OpenVPN are > required to reach my network. Know what I do too? I also have only three available IP addresses. I found this out when I tried to connect my newest laptop and it wouldn't assign me an IP address. I'd forgotten that I only .100 and .101 available. Now I have .100 through .102 available (and taken). No sense in having ip addresses available when no one *should* be using them. The iwl drivers seems to have done the trick!
text:

Now I have .100 through .102 available (and taken). No sense in having ip addresses available when no one *should* be using them. The iwl drivers seems to have done the trick!
